meta.title: Changsha Zhixike Technology Co., Ltd. | Software Products, AI Applications & Digital Platforms
meta.description: Changsha Zhixike Technology Co., Ltd. (XICO AI) builds software products, AI applications, web and mobile products, and digital platforms that are clear, reliable, and built to keep improving.

hero.subtitle: XICO is a software technology company. We build our own long-term products, and we help companies and founders move from requirement shaping and interface design to development and launch. Good technology does not need to be loud. It needs to be precise, durable, and worth using again.
hero.stack: Software Development · AI Applications · Web Platforms · Mobile Apps · Digital Systems

capabilities.title: From idea to launch, we make the middle real
capabilities.subtitle: We do not hand clients a pile of technology. We clarify the problem, then build systems that run, hold up, and keep room for the next version.

items.title: AI Application Delivery
items.desc: We place model capability inside real workflows: knowledge bases, support, content operations, retrieval, evaluation, and permission control.

items.title: Web and Mobile Products
items.desc: From customer-facing product to admin operations, we shape the core journey, visual system, and engineering structure together.

items.title: Business Systems and Platforms
items.desc: Orders, memberships, content, payments, data, and operation consoles designed around the way the business actually works.

items.title: Long-term Technical Foundations
items.desc: Clear architecture, maintainable code, deployment, monitoring, security boundaries, and performance work that reduce costly rework.

work.title: Our own products set the bar for our delivery
work.subtitle: Machi, Shangence, and our upcoming study-abroad and cross-border lines are long-term projects under Changsha Zhixike Technology Co., Ltd.

items.name: Machi
items.tagline: A city map for people living abroad
items.desc: A local life platform for students, new arrivals, and people learning a city.
items.about: Machi is built for people living abroad: students, new immigrants, and anyone still learning how a city works. City information, community, housing, jobs, second-hand listings, local services, and lived experience are brought into one place, so an unfamiliar city becomes easier to understand and easier to reach.

items.name: Shangence
items.tagline: Before opening a shop, understand the risk
items.desc: A risk-assessment and action-planning tool for small founders.
items.about: Shangence helps ordinary founders think before they spend. It reviews industry conditions, capital safety, personal capability, acquisition, and external constraints, then returns a risk score, validation budget, stop-loss line, and 30-day action plan. It does not promise success. It helps the decision become clearer.

items.name: Businesses in incubation
items.tagline: Extending product capability into specific industries
items.desc: We are developing product lines for study abroad, cross-border commerce, and local services across China, Japan, and overseas markets.
items.about: Study abroad, cross-border commerce, and local services are the next fields XICO will continue to explore. We want to bring software, design, and operations into specific industries, and build products people actually use, return to, and recommend.

approach.title: Good software is not made by piling things on
approach.subtitle: We put taste, engineering, and business judgment at the same table.

principles.title: Clarify the problem first
principles.desc: Requirements are not a list. They are a set of tradeoffs across users, cost, timing, and scope.

principles.title: Bring order to the interface
principles.desc: Design is not decoration. Hierarchy, feedback, and brand tone all affect trust.

principles.title: Write code for tomorrow
principles.desc: Clear structure and boundaries make a project easier to maintain, hand over, and grow.

principles.title: Think beyond one market
principles.desc: Chinese, Japanese, English, and cross-market realities belong in the plan from day one.

cta.title: If there is a product to build, start by making the problem clear.
cta.subtitle: Bring us an idea, an old system, or a business preparing to launch. We will help decide what to build, what to build first, and what can wait.

footer.tagline: Software products, AI applications, and digital platforms. We make complex work clear, and useful systems last.

about.title: Product, engineering, and taste in the same room.
about.subtitle: Changsha Zhixike Technology Co., Ltd. operates product development and software services under XICO AI｜智希可.
story: We started in Changsha and work toward China, Japan, and wider overseas markets. We build our own products, and we build software systems that clients can actually use.
We care about technology, but we do not worship it. A product becomes real through clear workflows, a trustworthy experience, stable engineering, and the ability to keep operating.
So the way we work is straightforward: ask the right questions, keep the interface clean, and build the system properly. Less showmanship, more long-term use.

items.title: A product needs a clear reason
items.desc: Not every idea should be built. We judge value before we add complexity.

items.title: Experience is a form of trust
items.desc: Good-looking is not enough. Clear, restrained, and easy to use is what makes people feel safe.

items.title: Engineering must survive the next step
items.desc: After launch come maintenance, expansion, fixes, and growth. The code has to leave room for tomorrow.

founder.name: Yao Kai · 姚凱
founder.quote: A product is not a screenshot of inspiration. It is a system people can use again and again.
bio: I started XICO to bring technology, design, and business judgment together. Many projects fail not because there is too little code, but because the first problem was never made clear.

I tend to look at products from a full-stack point of view: front end, mobile, back end, database, payments, admin, deployment, and operations all have to connect. If one part breaks, the user simply feels that the product is unreliable.

Changsha Zhixike Technology Co., Ltd. will keep building its own products, while opening its software development and product capability to collaborators. I hope it becomes a quiet company with real weight.

contact.subtitle: You do not need a polished brief. Tell us what is happening now, who it is for, and when it needs to go live. We will help work out the next step.

process.title: From first conversation to stable launch
process.subtitle: The process is not complicated, but each step has to be clean.

steps.title: Diagnosis and tradeoffs
steps.desc: We clarify goals, users, budget, timing, and the core functions that must ship.

steps.title: Prototype and plan
steps.desc: We map workflows, screens, and the technical route so risks appear early.

steps.title: Build and integrate
steps.desc: Front end, back end, database, permissions, payments, and deployment move forward by version, with clear acceptance points.

steps.title: Launch and iterate
steps.desc: After launch, we read data and feedback, fix what needs fixing, and leave the product in a sustainable operating state.

faq.title: A few things to clarify before working together
faq.subtitle: If yours isn't here, just write and ask.

items.q: Are you more of a software development company or a product studio?
items.a: Both. XICO builds long-term products of its own, and also opens its product, design, development, and launch capability to external collaborations.

items.q: Can we talk if the idea is still rough?
items.a: Yes. Many projects start incomplete. We will first help judge whether the need is real, how small the first version should be, and which features can wait.

items.q: What kinds of projects do you usually take on?
items.a: Software products, AI applications, web and mobile products, admin systems, digital platforms, company websites, and brand experience.

items.q: How long does a project usually take?
items.a: It depends on scope. A usable first version usually takes weeks to a few months. We prefer to make the first version clear and solid, then iterate.

items.q: Do you support the product after launch?
items.a: Yes. Deployment, monitoring, fixes, feature iteration, content, and data maintenance can continue as the project needs.
Send an email or fill out the contact form. Tell us what you want to build, where the project stands, and the expected timing. We usually reply within 1-2 business days.

manifesto.title: Less noise. More weight.
body: We like products that do not rush to prove themselves: clear when opened, smooth when used, responsible when something goes wrong, and still able to grow years later.
A website can be beautiful and the code can be new, but what remains is whether the system helped someone get the work done.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Changsha Zhixike Technology Co., Ltd. (“XICO AI”, “we”) respects and protects your privacy. This policy explains what information we collect on xicoai.com, how we use it, and the rights you have.

sections.p: When you contact us through a form, a partnership enquiry or a job application, we collect the information you choose to provide — such as your name, email, company, message, and the résumé file you upload when applying. We also collect basic technical logs (time of visit, pages, device type) to keep the site secure and stable.

We use this information only to respond to your enquiries, process collaboration or job applications, and operate and improve the site. We do not sell your personal information or use it for unrelated purposes.
Information is stored on servers under our control and protected with reasonable technical and organizational measures. We keep it only as long as needed for the purposes above.
We use only essential local storage to remember your theme (light/dark) and language preference. We do not use it for cross-site tracking.
You can contact us at hi@xicoai.com at any time to access, correct or delete your personal information.
We may update this policy from time to time. Material changes will be posted on this page. Questions? Email hi@xicoai.com.

terms.title: Terms of Service

Unless otherwise stated, the text, design, marks and content of this site belong to XICO AI and may not be copied or used commercially without permission. Third-party brand marks belong to their respective owners.
Content on this site is provided “as is”. We aim to keep it accurate but make no warranty as to its completeness or timeliness.
To the extent permitted by law, XICO AI is not liable for any indirect or incidental loss arising from use of this site.
These terms are governed by the laws of the People's Republic of China.
For any questions about these terms, contact hi@xicoai.com.
